mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already low, discount rates ease and equity valuation multiples expand. This mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because cheaper policy funding supports risk assets and reduces pressure on future cash flows.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is declining for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if the sign of the return relation flips negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A threshold crossing above 4.5% in FEDFUNDS signals restrictive policy that raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions. After that threshold is breached, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days should be negative as equities reprice to higher financing costs.
failure_condition: Reject if post-threshold 5-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of FEDFUNDS > 4.5% episodes, or if the average 5-day return after the threshold is not lower than unconditional average by at least 50 bps.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized deterioration in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates weakening nominal demand and labor income. That combination should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month because lower sales and payroll growth reduce revenue expectations and earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of RSAFS growth slowing and PAYEMS growth slowing does not coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns more often than 55% of the time, or if either series alone explains the full effect without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, the labor market is losing momentum and forward earnings expectations weaken. This should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month as investors price slower growth and higher recession odds.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in a majority of episodes where UNRATE has risen more than 0.3pp over three months, or if the signal loses predictive power after controlling for market trend.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial output and cyclical demand for goods. Because that usually precedes softer earnings and lower cyclical risk appetite, the S&P 500 should underperform over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not lower after 3-month declines in INDPRO, or if the relationship is not observable in at least two distinct slowdown episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below its recent trend indicates housing-sector demand compression, which typically propagates to consumer spending and cyclical equities. That should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when housing weakness is pronounced.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not negative following housing-trend breaks, or if the effect disappears after excluding periods with simultaneous labor-market weakening.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment, a falling CPIAUCSL combined with stable or easing FEDFUNDS raises real policy accommodation and supports risk assets. The mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because real rates should decline or stay contained.
failure_condition: Reject if combined declines in CPIAUCSL and flat-to-lower FEDFUNDS do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal turns negative during disinflation episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A low or falling UNRATE together with rising PAYEMS indicates resilient household income and broad labor demand. That combination should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because earnings and consumption expectations remain firm.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor-strength condition fails to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered instances, or if PAYEMS alone fully explains the effect without UNRATE interaction.
